steps.title: Connect Knight to fast painting
steps.content: Knight is named among Hair's acolytes who imitated fast painting, a technique involving multiple canvases at once. The passage says this technique helped create impressionistic qualities now associated with the Florida Highwaymen's shared aesthetic, while Reagan's more detailed paintings are presented as a partial exception. This explains why Knight's work would likely look closer to the group's typical style.

steps.title: Reject unsupported judgments
steps.content: Choice B invents a claim about Knight being less derivative than people think. Choice C turns the impressionistic quality into inferiority, which the text never does. Choice D says Knight's paintings are more aesthetically interesting than Reagan's, another value judgment absent from the passage. The answer is A.
